title: Исследовать несовместимость OpenClaw latest с live Clawdiy
description:
## Контекст
Официальный upgrade rollout Clawdiy на `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` завершился деградацией live health в run `23090853145`, после чего потребовался rollback на `2026.3.11`.

## Что нужно сделать
- определить, какой именно OpenClaw build скрывается за `latest` и почему он не вышел в healthy на нашей runtime topology
- проверить official release notes и official issues на уже известный риск
- подобрать следующий безопасный upgrade candidate и protocol canary
- подготовить reproducible acceptance plan перед новым production update

## Acceptance Criteria
- зафиксирован конкретный следующий upgrade candidate
- есть documented canary protocol
- есть решение, можно ли переводить tracked default с `2026.3.11` на новую версию
notes:
- 2026-03-14: official GitHub release latest = `v2026.3.13`, but official Docker `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` currently resolves to image label `org.opencontainers.image.version=2026.3.12` and digest `sha256:c871ddf7ad1b4125218e5f010b59724f0d2bb299714c5bd6ba7c1e99462450a7`.
- exact Docker tag `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:2026.3.13` still returns `manifest unknown`.
- clean production update attempt via workflow run `23091734325` pulled `latest`, container stayed `starting` ~110s, then became `unhealthy`, and auto-rollback restored `2026.3.11`.
- separate server GitOps blocker was also found and preserved in stash `pre-latest-update-20260314-162243`; tracked drift matched already-merged `main` changes, plus untracked `config/oauth_tokens.json`.

title: Fix rca-index.sh octal parsing for zero-padded RCA IDs
description: Problem: rca-index.sh update fails on existing RCA IDs with leading zeros like 008 and 009 because bash numeric comparison treats them as octal. Evidence: during RCA-012 landing on 2026-03-12 the command printed value too great for base errors for 009 and 008, and docs/rca/INDEX.md had to be updated manually. Expected: rca-index.sh update and rca-index.sh validate both exit 0 and rebuild docs/rca/INDEX.md without octal parsing warnings for zero-padded RCA IDs.
notes: Acceptance: update exits 0; validate exits 0; no octal parsing warnings remain for zero-padded RCA IDs.

title: Предотвратить server-side drift от config/oauth_tokens.json у Clawdiy
description:
## Контекст
Во время повторной попытки production update Clawdiy workflow сначала был заблокирован GitOps guard из-за грязного `/opt/moltinger`. После разбора выяснилось, что кроме ожидаемых tracked-изменений на сервере появился untracked файл `config/oauth_tokens.json` с runtime OAuth-данными. Это не должно появляться внутри git checkout и ломает автоматический repair path.

## Что нужно сделать
- определить, какой процесс или шаг создает `/opt/moltinger/config/oauth_tokens.json`
- вынести runtime OAuth token store из git checkout или исключить его из server-side drift surface
- обновить operator runbook и/или deploy automation так, чтобы production update не блокировался на этом артефакте

## Риски
- повторная блокировка deploy/update workflow
- смешение runtime секретов и GitOps checkout
- риск ручной потери OAuth state при неаккуратной очистке сервера
acceptance_criteria:
- зафиксирован источник появления `config/oauth_tokens.json`
- runtime OAuth token store больше не попадает в `/opt/moltinger` как untracked drift
- повторный deploy workflow не блокируется этим артефактом

title: Redeploy Clawdiy writable runtime-home fix and rerun official OpenClaw wizard
description:
## Context
The official `openclaw onboard` Codex OAuth flow on live `clawdiy` completed OAuth but failed on `EACCES` because the deployed runtime mounted only a read-only `openclaw.json` file instead of a writable `~/.openclaw` runtime home. Branch `022-clawdiy-wizard-writability-fix` prepares the code-side fix.

## Required work
- merge and deploy branch `022-clawdiy-wizard-writability-fix`
- verify `/opt/moltinger/data/clawdiy/runtime` is owned by `1000:1000` on `ainetic.tech`
- rerun the official wizard from the existing SSH session path
- confirm `codex-oauth/gpt-5.4` is persisted after the wizard completes
- run live post-checks for `models status`, dashboard usability, and first chat probe

## Why this matters
Without the rollout, the repository fix exists but live Clawdiy still remains incompatible with the official OpenClaw wizard/auth lifecycle.

title: fix(telegram): replace allowed_users with dm_policy + allowlist
description: Telegram bot sends verification code to ALL users including owner (ID 262872984). Root cause: 'allowed_users' is not a recognized Moltis parameter - it is silently ignored by the TOML parser. Moltis docs (channels.html) show the correct format: dm_policy = allowlist + allowlist = [...]. Fix: rename allowed_users -> dm_policy + allowlist in config/moltis.toml:378-381. Verified by consilium of 19 experts.
close_reason: Fixed: config/moltis.toml updated - replaced allowed_users with dm_policy=allowlist + allowlist=[262872984]. Committed in 9062f00, pushed to main, CI/CD will deploy automatically.

Выкатить cleanup legacy Codex Telegram UX и проверить снятие старой клавиатуры
description:
## Контекст
В ветке 023-full-moltis-codex-update-skill новый Moltis-native scheduler теперь шлёт ReplyKeyboardRemove, а legacy watcher принудительно переведён в one-way режим без /codex_da и /codex_net. Эти изменения ещё нужно выкатить в production runtime.

## Что нужно
1. Вкатить изменения ветки после merge в production Moltis runtime.
2. Спровоцировать новое Telegram-уведомление от Moltis-native codex-update skill.
3. Подтвердить, что старая reply keyboard с /codex_da и /codex_net исчезла у пользователя.
4. Повторить live UAT для запроса про обновления Codex CLI уже без legacy Telegram UX residue.

## Почему отдельно
Кодовая часть cleanup завершена в feature-ветке, но визуальный эффект в Telegram появится только после rollout и нового bot message с ReplyKeyboardRemove.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Production Moltis отправляет новое Codex update уведомление с remove_keyboard.
2. У пользователя больше не отображается старая reply keyboard с /codex_da и /codex_net.
3. Live Telegram UAT по запросу про обновления Codex CLI проходит без legacy UX residue.
4. Если новый live run всё ещё падает по timeout, это фиксируется уже как отдельная execution/debug задача, не как keyboard-residue bug.

## Goal

- Deploy SearXNG via Docker Compose on same server as Moltis
- Configure as MCP server or HTTP endpoint for Moltis
- Aggregate multiple search engines (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, etc.)
- No rate limits, no API keys, no credit card

## Technical Notes

- SearXNG GitHub: https://github.com/searxng/searxng
- Can be added to docker-compose.prod.yml alongside Moltis
- Expose on internal port, integrate via MCP or custom tool

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SearXNG container running on ainetic.tech
- [ ] Web search working through SearXNG

fix(ci): Add concurrency control to prevent parallel deploys
description:
## Problem
No concurrency control in deploy.yml. Multiple simultaneous pushes can cause race conditions and broken deploys.

## Solution
Add concurrency group to .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
```yaml
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] concurrency.group added to workflow
- [ ] Second deploy waits for first to complete
- [ ] No more race conditions

**Effort**: 30min
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: CI/CD Architect
close_reason: Fixed: Added concurrency.group to deploy.yml. Parallel deployments now queue instead of race.

title: fix(security): Remove privileged mode from moltis container
description:
## Problem
moltis container runs with privileged: true. Container escape = host compromise.

## Current (DANGEROUS):
```yaml
privileged: true
```

## Solution
Replace with fine-grained capabilities:
```yaml
cap_add:
  - SYS_ADMIN
security_opt:
  - seccomp:unconfined
```
OR use gVisor/Kata Containers for sandbox.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] privileged: true removed
- [ ] Container still functions correctly
- [ ] Sandbox operations work

**Effort**: 4h
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: Docker Engineer

- only pursue rollout if analysis shows clear value

- document pros/cons of polling vs webhook for current Moltinger production usage
- quantify current pain/limits of polling (latency, resource usage, missed messages, operational burden)
- identify exact prerequisites for safe webhook migration
- produce explicit recommendation: keep polling or migrate to webhook

- written decision memo exists with recommendation and rationale
- recommendation includes rollback/operational impact if migration is approved
- if recommendation is to stay on polling, webhook rollout work is formally deprioritized

ops: controlled Telegram webhook rollout after polling stabilization
notes:
Progress 2026-03-07: polling stabilization codified in git (commit c724368, branch codex/fix-bot). Webhook monitor/enforcement removed; telegram allowlist normalized to string IDs. Remaining scope: design and validate webhook endpoint contract before re-enabling webhook in prod.
Progress 2026-03-07: added controlled rollout tooling (scripts/telegram-webhook-rollout.sh + workflow telegram-webhook-rollout.yml + runbook docs/telegram-webhook-rollout.md), optional deploy secret propagation (TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL/TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET), and manifest registration. External probe snapshot on 2026-03-07 shows POST /telegram/webhook and /telegram-webhook return 303 -> /login, so webhook cutover remains blocked until non-redirect callback endpoint is confirmed.
Related work moved to new Speckit epic moltinger-xtx (On-demand Telegram E2E Harness). This relation is contextual only; not parent/child.
Progress 2026-03-08: merged webhook/user-monitor backfill into origin/main (01da067) and merged origin/main into codex/full-review. Remote smoke on ainetic.tech via server-side execution of current telegram-webhook-monitor.sh shows bot token valid and probe send works, but webhook remains disabled: getWebhookInfo.url empty, inbound_mode=polling, pending_update_count=0. Server checkout /opt/moltinger is still at 65c9942 and does not yet contain telegram-webhook-monitor.sh in-tree, so current tooling is not deployed there. Telegram Web real-user probe using existing /opt/moltinger/data/.telegram-web-state.json passed: sent text "test2", received reply "Test2". Remaining work: deploy latest main to server checkout and complete controlled webhook cutover only after endpoint contract is confirmed.
Progress 2026-03-08: performed safe drift remediation on server checkout by stashing server-only changes/untracked files (stash message pre-deploy-drift-20260308T170318Z), fetching origin, and fast-forwarding /opt/moltinger main to 01da067. Triggered Deploy Moltis workflow_dispatch run 22825679407 on main@01da067; deploy and post-deployment verification both succeeded. Server audit markers now show .deployed-sha=01da06721e62994b6f221a919dc3c4334048fa97 and workflow_run=22825679407. Post-deploy smoke: scripts/telegram-webhook-monitor.sh --json executes on server but still fails because Telegram remains in polling mode (getWebhookInfo.url empty, inbound_mode=polling). Endpoint contract remains blocked: POST https://moltis.ainetic.tech/telegram/webhook and /telegram-webhook both return HTTP 303 Location: /login. Telegram Web user-mode monitor runs successfully after deploy, but observed reply correlation may be weak because it accepted a recent probe text from the same chat rather than a clearly attributable /status response.
Decision 2026-03-08: production remains on polling for now. Real-user Telegram interaction is confirmed working, so webhook rollout is no longer an immediate delivery requirement. Further work on webhook migration is deferred pending separate P2 analysis task: assess whether production should migrate from polling to webhook.

title: bug(beads): investigate and fix shared redirect tracker leakage across worktrees
description:
Context
- During forensic analysis of root main .beads/issues.jsonl, multiple parallel worktree issue families appeared mixed into one tracker state.
- live `bd worktree list` currently shows several worktrees as `redirect -> moltinger` instead of isolated/local Beads state.
- This makes it unclear which branch/worktree owns which issue state and can cause hidden tracker drift in root main.

Problem
- We need to identify the exact source of this redirect behavior and stop unintended cross-worktree tracker writes.
- Possible sources include Beads worktree redirect config, daemon behavior, local tracker bootstrap rules, merge-driver interactions, or project-specific topology automation.

Required
- Reproduce the redirect behavior on the current topology.
- Identify the exact mechanism that routes worktrees into the shared `moltinger` Beads context.
- Determine which behavior is intentional vs accidental.
- Define the expected ownership model for issue state per worktree/branch.
- Implement a fix so worktrees either stay isolated or redirect only when explicitly intended.
- Add guardrails or diagnostics so future silent leakage is detectable early.

Acceptance Criteria
- There is a written root-cause explanation for why `bd worktree list` shows `redirect -> moltinger` for affected worktrees.
- The fix makes worktree tracker ownership deterministic and understandable.
- Root main tracker no longer silently accumulates unrelated worktree issue-state.
- There is an explicit validation step or test proving the corrected behavior.
- Documentation explains the intended tracker model and how to diagnose drift.

title: fix(deploy): Replace sed -i with GitOps-compliant approach
description:
## Problem
scripts/deploy.sh rollback() uses 'sed -i' which violates GitOps principles. Causes configuration drift.

## Current Code (BAD):
```bash
sed -i "s|image:.*:.*|image:...$VERSION|"
```

## Solution
Use environment variables or full file sync:
- Replace sed with heredoc or envsubst
- OR sync entire file from git
- Ensure server state = git state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No sed -i in deploy scripts
- [ ] GitOps compliance verified
- [ ] Rollback still works

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: Unix Script Expert, GitOps Specialist

- Clawdiy workspace mount drift matches commit 2de6297 (`Fix Clawdiy workspace mount permissions`) and related branch line `017-clawdiy-workspace-mount-fix`.
- Beads/local-db drift matches merged-or-merge-adjacent line around 0f2afcb (`merge(main): reconcile plain-bd UX with beads recovery batch`) plus related worktree-local tracker files.
- Telegram web probe drift matches line around 1596b62 (`fix(telegram): harden telegram web send strategy`) and earlier remote-UAT hardening commits.

This suggests /opt/moltinger contains an unsafe manual mix of partially landed branch work, not one coherent uncommitted patch.
2026-03-13: Re-checked production host during advisory rollout preparation. /opt/moltinger no longer only shows dirty files; it now reports branch feat/moltinger-z8m-4-moltis-post-update-error-remediation at 9224c70 even after successful deploy workflow 23026415235 for main commit 7c6e98f. Treat this as the same server-side checkout drift family: GitOps reports success, but authoritative runtime checkout is not on main. Reconcile server checkout state before enabling Moltis-native advisory interactive mode live.

title: Moltis core must route Telegram command ingress before generic chat for Codex consent
description: Production evidence on 2026-03-12 showed that Telegram message '/codex_da' went directly into the generic agent loop instead of the repo-managed codex consent router. The consent record req-28eef72c remained pending while Moltis replied with a generic assistant message. Until Moltis core exposes Telegram command ingress to repo-managed hooks before generic handling, codex upstream watcher must stay in one-way alert mode. Acceptance for the real fix: a Telegram command or button action for a pending Codex consent request reaches router first, suppresses the generic chat response, resolves the shared consent store record, and sends immediate recommendations.

title: tests: tighten telegram web user monitor reply correlation
description:
Background: post-deploy remote smoke on 2026-03-08 showed scripts/telegram-web-user-monitor.sh returned status=pass, but the captured reply_text matched a recent webhook monitor probe from the same Telegram chat rather than an unambiguous response to the current /status probe. This can produce false-positive UAT results on active chats.

Required:
- correlate observed Telegram Web replies to the current probe message more strictly
- avoid passing on stale or unrelated recent bot messages in the same chat

Убрать false positive authoritative Telegram UAT на промежуточных ответах
description:
## Контекст
Live Telegram UAT по запросу "Проверь обновления Codex CLI" мог ставить authoritative verdict=passed слишком рано: probe принимал первый входящий ответ после отправки сообщения, даже если затем бот завершал диалог timeout-карточкой `Timed out: Agent run timed out after 30s`.

## Что нужно
1. Не считать первый входящий bubble окончательным успехом.
2. Дождаться стабилизации ответа через короткое окно тишины после первого attributable reply.
3. Оценивать verdict по последнему стабильному ответу, а не по ранней промежуточной реплике.

## Acceptance Criteria
- authoritative Telegram Web probe не ставит pass по ранней промежуточной реплике, если следом приходит timeout/error reply;
- есть component coverage на сценарий `Проверяю... -> Timed out...`;
- runbook явно описывает новый settle-window contract.

title: Развернуть codex consent routing на Moltis runtime
description:
Нужно довести live Telegram consent flow до рабочего server-side состояния.
